description_long: This is a small, intricately detailed bronze sculpture featuring two standing figures, likely of Buddhist origin, set against a teardrop-shaped mandorla with ornate swirling patterns. The figures are positioned side by side, each standing on a circular pedestal, and are adorned with flowing robes and delicate headpieces. The mandorla is richly textured, with engraved motifs that enhance the spiritual and artistic presence of the piece. The sculpture is mounted on a rectangular, four-legged base, which is sturdy and slightly weathered, suggesting age and historical significance. The back of the mandorla is plain and shows signs of patina, while one leg of the base has inscribed characters, possibly denoting provenance or dedication. The overall finish is matte with a greenish-brown patina, indicative of aged bronze.
description: A small bronze sculpture of two robed figures set against an ornate teardrop-shaped mandorla, mounted on a four-legged base with inscribed characters and a weathered patina.
description_view_0: Front view showing both standing figures, detailed robes, headpieces, and the ornate mandorla with swirling patterns.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the sculpture, the depth of the figures, and the layered construction of the mandorla and base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, patinated back of the mandorla and the inscribed characters on the base's leg.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the slimness of the sculpture and the three-dimensional relief of the figures.
attribute_subject_matter: two standing Buddhist figures with a decorative mandorla
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
